title: Pollia crispata
taxonomy: Pollia crispata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. It belongs to the genus Pollia, which consists of several species of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Commelinaceae family is a group of monocotyledonous plants, often referred to as spiderworts or dayflowers. Pollia crispata is a type of herbaceous plant with distinctive features, including its waxy texture and unique seed coat. By classifying Pollia crispata within the Commelinaceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Pollia crispata, commonly known as the 'crinkle-leaf' or 'Polynesian taro', is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, preventing infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pollia crispata include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
